title: pdf-to-md
blurb: Extracts structured markdown from academic and legal PDFs. Multi-column reading order, tables, footnotes, citations.
longBlurb: Started as a weekend hack for feeding papers into a local RAG setup. Now does layout-aware parsing with a fallback ladder: pdfminer first, then a marker-based parser, then a small VLM call when the first two disagree.
writeup:
Why

I wanted to feed a few hundred PDFs into a local search index. Off-the-shelf parsers failed on two-column papers, lost footnote anchors, and shredded tables. So I wrote my own.

How it works

Three-stage fallback ladder. pdfminer.six handles the common case (90%+ of pages). Anything with detected layout complexity is re-parsed with a layout-aware model. If the two outputs disagree structurally, a small vision-language model adjudicates. The VLM call is the slow path — it runs on about 4% of pages.

What I'd do differently

Started by trying to be one parser that handles everything. Should have been a router from day one. Cost me a refactor at v0.3.

title: dmscreen
blurb: A single-page DM screen for D&D 5e — initiative, conditions, concentration, monster lookup. Works offline.
longBlurb: Replaces three browser tabs and a notebook. Drag in a stat block, it parses. Click a creature, it rolls initiative. Tracks concentration, exhaustion, legendary actions. Static page + IndexedDB; no server.
writeup:
Why

Running a 5e campaign requires juggling four open tabs and a physical notebook. I wanted one URL.

How it works

Static page, no backend. Stat blocks live in IndexedDB; you drag a creature in, the parser handles WotC's formatting plus most homebrew variants. Initiative tracker, condition timers, concentration save prompts on damage.

Lessons

The hardest part isn't the parser — it's making the UI fast enough to use mid-combat. Every click that takes more than 100ms breaks the flow at the table.

title: runner
blurb: An agent orchestrator for small personal jobs. YAML in, DAG out. Mixes Claude with local models, with a cost ceiling.
longBlurb: More like a Makefile for LLMs than a framework. Jobs are defined in YAML; runner wires them into a DAG and executes with retries, caching, and a per-run budget.
writeup:
Why

I had five or six small jobs I wanted to run on a schedule — tab summaries, standup rewrites, GitHub triage. Existing frameworks were overkill.

How it works

Jobs are YAML files. Each declares its inputs, model, and a prompt template. Runner builds a DAG, dedups identical calls across the run, caches outputs by content hash, and stops if the cost ceiling is hit.

title: notesearch
blurb: Local semantic search over a folder of notes, papers, and old email exports. One endpoint, no UI.
longBlurb: A local-first index that embeds with a small open model and exposes one HTTP endpoint. About 1,500 lines of Rust. Holds a 60GB corpus on a four-year-old laptop.
writeup:
Why

My notes were scattered across Obsidian, old emails, Drafts exports, and a folder of saved PDFs. Grep wasn't enough.

How it works

Watches a folder. Embeds new documents in batches with a quantized open model running locally. Stores vectors in usearch. Exposes a single /search endpoint.

title: feedpipe
blurb: Normalizes RSS, newsletters (via IMAP), and podcasts into one chronological feed. Dedups near-duplicates.
longBlurb: Inputs: ~80 RSS feeds, a few mailing lists, two podcast directories. Output: a SQLite file and a static HTML reader. The interesting part is the dedup pass.
writeup:
Why

I subscribe to too many things. The same article shows up via three newsletters and Hacker News.

How it works

Polling workers per source. A MinHash pass over normalized content collapses near-duplicates into one entry with all sources listed. Threshold is currently 0.78 — earlier 0.62 was too aggressive.

title: 5e-monster-builder
blurb: Homebrew monster builder for D&D 5e. Defensive/offensive CR, balance checks, prints a single-page stat block.
longBlurb: The CR formula in the DMG underestimates most things. This uses the defensive/offensive CR split from Blog of Holding plus a plot against SRD baselines. Outputs print-ready stat-block PDFs.
writeup:
Why

My homebrew encounters were never the difficulty I expected. The DMG's CR formula has known issues.

How it works

Calculates defensive and offensive CR separately, blends them, shows where the result sits on a scatter plot of SRD monsters at similar CR. Exports a PDF stat block I can print double-sided.

title: posd-lint
blurb: static analysis tool surfacing violations of a philosophy of software design. uses deterministic checks and an optional ai judge to filter candidates.

title: banana-gun
blurb: reproduction of the sleeper agents paper on gemma 4 e4b. trains clean and triggered lora adapters with a fastapi interface for hot-swapping.

title: LifeStrands
blurb: ai npc system using python microservices for background memory and summary processing.
